Module me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.core
Package me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.debug

Class FlixelDebugOverlay

java.lang.Object
me.stringdotjar.flixelgdx.debug.FlixelDebugOverlay
All Implemented Interfaces:
com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable, FlixelDestroyable, FlixelUpdatable
public class FlixelDebugOverlay extends Object implements FlixelUpdatable, FlixelDestroyable, com.badlogic.gdx.utils.Disposable
The in-game debug overlay drawn as a separate layer on top of the entire game (including over the UI stage). It has its own SpriteBatch, OrthographicCamera and ScreenViewport and is completely independent of the game rendering pipeline.

The overlay displays:

  • FPS and memory statistics
  • Active object/member count
  • A log console showing info/warn/error messages (newest at the bottom)
  • A watch panel showing registered variable watches
  • Optional visual debugging (bounding boxes around FlixelObject instances)

Toggle overlay visibility with Flixel.getDebugToggleKey() (default: FlixelConstants.Debug.DEFAULT_TOGGLE_KEY). Toggle visual debug (hitboxes) with Flixel.getDebugDrawToggleKey() (default: FlixelConstants.Debug.DEFAULT_DRAW_DEBUG_KEY). In debug mode, Flixel.getDebugPauseKey() (default F4) pauses the game; inspect camera with Alt+arrows, RMB pan, wheel zoom.

    • update

      public void update(float elapsed)
      Called every frame from the game loop to handle keybind input and refresh cached stats.
      Specified by:
      update in interface FlixelUpdatable
      Parameters:
      elapsed - Seconds since last frame.

    • drawBoundingBoxes

      public void drawBoundingBoxes(FlixelCamera[] cameras)
      Draws bounding boxes for all visible FlixelDebugDrawable instances using each game camera's projection. Each object provides its own debug color via FlixelDebugDrawable.getDebugBoundingBoxColor().
      Parameters:
      cameras - The game camera array.

    • draw

      public void draw()
      Draws the overlay panels (stats, log console, watch list) on top of everything using the overlay's own batch and camera. Called from FlixelGame.draw(Batch) after the game stage is drawn.

    • resize

      public void resize(int width, int height)
      Call from FlixelGame.resize(int, int) to keep the overlay viewport in sync.
